metadata.title:
TRY TO SMASH


UNION DEPOT

Railroads Don't Want Project and
Are Trying to Bluff City Out


of the Idea.

pages.raw_text:
Interviews with James m. Walsh,
rieht-of-way man and local repre-
sentative of the Milwaukee, and
Robert E. Strahorn, president of the
North Coast, as to the attitude of
the two companies on the union
depot matter, confirms, In meas
ure, the statement printed In The
Press few days ago that move
Is on foot to defeat the building OF
the proposed union terminal.


Neither Walsh nor Strahorn
would make positive statement as
to whether the union passenger sta
tion would or would not be built
They. with one accord, said that II
depended on the sort of franchise
given them by the city council. II
the council recedes from the de.
mand for a union depot or insists
on grades that the railroads consid
er impracticable in reaching joint
terminal then the railroads will
gladly ask to be saved the expense
of the $l,000,000 station called fol
in the Shaefer amendment.


From reliable sources comes the
report that the Milwaukee and the
North Coast, as slap TO the city
for the delay that has ensued In
passing the franchises, and to be
relieved of a situation that was
never one OF pleasure, will use ev
cry effort to defeat the building ot
